vulnerability.title: Ensure PAM Enforces Password Requirements - Enforce for root User
vulnerability.description: The pam_pwquality module's enforce_for_root parameter controls requirements for enforcing password complexity for the root user. Enable the enforce_for_root setting in /etc/security/pwquality.conf to require the root user to use complex passwords.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { rpm --quiet -q libpwquality; }; then if [ -e "/etc/security/pwquality.conf" ] ; then LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*enforce_for_root/Id" "/etc/security/pwquality.conf" else touch "/etc/security/pwquality.conf" fi # make sure file has newline at the end sed -i -e '$a\' "/etc/security/pwquality.conf" cp "/etc/security/pwquality.conf" "/etc/security/pwquality.conf.bak" # Insert at the end of the file printf '%s\n' "enforce_for_root" >> "/etc/security/pwquality.conf" # Clean up after ourselves. rm "/etc/security/pwquality.conf.bak" else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: Enabling enforce_for_root in /etc/security/pwquality.conf is a standard PAM password policy change that does not affect system boot, networking, or file system integrity. It only enforces password complexity for the root account, which is a common hardening step and unlikely to cause lockout or service disruption.

llm_verdict.message: The remediation has been completed. The file `/etc/security/pwquality.conf` now has `enforce_for_root = 1`. The original file was backed up as `/etc/security/pwquality.conf.bak`.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan correctly enables enforce_for_root in /etc/security/pwquality.conf and is backwards‑compatible, but could be tightened up for robustness.
concerns: Sed pattern may match unintended lines (e.g., comments or different parameters containing 'enforce_for_root').
If the file contains multiple instances of the setting, only the first will be rewritten, leaving others unchanged or duplicated.
The plan does not preserve the original permission and ownership of the file after writing.
suggested_improvements: Use a stricter regex such as '^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*enforce_for_root[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*.*$' to avoid accidental matches.
Ensure the backup copy preserves mode, ownership, and ACLs, e.g., cp --preserve=mode,ownership,context /etc/security/pwquality.conf /etc/security/pwquality.conf.bak.
Add idempotency checks: first search for existing 'enforce_for_root' line, only add if absent, and verify the value after edit before re‑write.
Add a comment line indicating the change and rationale for future reference.
Explicitly set the correct file permissions (e.g., 600) after writing, if they can be altered by the script.
